root_text.an7.16:1.1: “Sattime, bhikkhave, puggalā āhuneyyā pāhuneyyā dakkhiṇeyyā añjalikaraṇīyā anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.16:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī viharati, aniccasaññī, aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.16:1.4: So āsavānaṁ khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.16:1.5: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo puggalo āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.16:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī viharati, aniccasaññī, aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.16:2.2: Tassa apubbaṁ acarimaṁ āsavapariyādānañca hoti jīvitapariyādānañca.
root_text.an7.16:2.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.16:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī viharati, aniccasaññī, aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.16:3.2: So pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti …pe…
root_text.an7.16:3.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, sattamo puggalo āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.16:3.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, satta puggalā āhuneyyā pāhuneyyā dakkhiṇeyyā añjalikaraṇīyā anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an7.16:1.1: “Mendicants, these seven individuals are worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and are the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an7.16:1.3: First, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in all conditions. They perceive impermanence and experience impermanence. Constantly, continually, and without interruption, they apply the mind and fathom with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.16:1.4: They’ve realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an7.16:2.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in all conditions.
translation_text.an7.16:2.2: Their defilements and their life come to an end at exactly the same time.
translation_text.an7.16:3.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in all conditions.
translation_text.an7.16:3.2: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished between one life and the next. …
translation_text.an7.16:3.3: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished upon landing. …
translation_text.an7.16:3.4: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished without extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.16:3.5: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished with extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.16:3.6: With the ending of the five lower fetters they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm.
translation_text.an7.16:3.8: These are the seven individuals who are worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and are the supreme field of merit for the world.”
